evals.name: ec2-linux-nginx-logs
evals.prompt: I have an EC2 Linux box running nginx and I want the cloudwatch agent to ship CPU, memory, disk usage, and tail /var/log/nginx/access.log and /var/log/nginx/error.log into CloudWatch Logs. Give me a config and the IAM policy I'll need. 14-day retention on the access log is fine.
evals.expected_output: A valid amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json with an `agent` block, `metrics.metrics_collected.{cpu,mem,disk}`, `logs.logs_collected.files.collect_list` for both nginx files with log_group_name and retention_in_days set, plus a least-privilege IAM policy.

evals.name: eks-prometheus-emf
evals.prompt: Setting up the cloudwatch agent on EKS to scrape a Spring Boot app's /actuator/prometheus endpoint. I want jvm_memory_bytes_used and http_server_requests_seconds_count to show up as actual CloudWatch metrics (not just log lines) with ClusterName and Namespace dimensions. Cluster is called 'prod-eks-1'.
evals.expected_output: Config with `logs.metrics_collected.prometheus` including prometheus_config_path, cluster_name='prod-eks-1', and an emf_processor with metric_declaration entries naming the two metrics with [ClusterName, Namespace] dimensions. Mention of the separate Prometheus YAML file the user needs (kubernetes_sd_configs).

evals.name: windows-event-logs
evals.prompt: Need the cloudwatch agent on a Windows 2019 server to ship the System and Application event logs (warnings and errors only) plus disk free % and memory committed % perfcounters. Give me the json and where it goes.
evals.expected_output: Config with `metrics.metrics_collected.LogicalDisk` (% Free Space) and `metrics.metrics_collected.Memory` (% Committed Bytes In Use), plus `logs.logs_collected.windows_events.collect_list` for System and Application with event_levels = [WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL]. Tell user to put it at C:\ProgramData\Amazon\AmazonCloudWatchAgent\amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json.

evals.name: statsd-procstat-combo
evals.prompt: Linux EC2. I want the cloudwatch agent to: (1) listen on UDP 8125 for statsd from my app, and (2) watch the postgres process and report its CPU and memory. Custom namespace 'MyApp/Prod'.
evals.expected_output: Config with `metrics.namespace = 'MyApp/Prod'`, `metrics_collected.statsd` with service_address ':8125', and `metrics_collected.procstat` with either pid_file or exe='postgres' and measurement including cpu_usage and memory_rss.
